filename: Instructions for the use of the Instrument maintenance at Biomedicum 1 and 2u.docx

full_text:
Instrument maintenance instructions for Biomedicum 1 and 2U

We handle all our services only through the service points. Please don’t bring any products directly to the ground floor equipment service.

If you have any wishes regarding the products, for example, you need certain products to be sterilized, please send a request to our joint email:

valinehuolto-meilahti@helsinki.fi

When you send us a message, please remember to tell us which lab you are working in, which floor your lab is located on (including which house), and your contact information so that we can contact you if needed.

Locations of the equipment service points

These instructions do not apply to the Folkhälsan Equipment maintenance unit on the 3rd floor with their instructions.

In the service points you will find:

Baskets for returning laboratory-owned equipment to you after washing and/or sterilization.

A point where you can bring dry things or liquids to be sterilized.

A point to which all the glassware will be returned.

A point to which sterilized products will be returned.

Point where you can get sterilization bags and autoclave tape for your use.

Shelves with clean general glassware for everyone’s use.

Only on long shelf time products foil will be added. Equipment maintenance personnel monitor the movement of the glassware.

If you are looking for common glassware that are not on the shelf, or you want more than what's on the shelf, please contact us at our email address.

In Biomedicum 1 service points are also available

Ice machines

Type 1 Ultra Pure Water Equipment (Milli-Q)

In Biomedicum 2U there are two separate rooms with:

Type 1 ultrapure water machines and ice machines

In rooms BP13a2 and S208.2.

Instructions for equipment maintenance users

We handle all our services only through the service points. Please don’t bring any products directly to the ground floor equipment service.

If you have any wishes regarding the products, for example, you need certain products to be sterilized, please send a request to our joint email:

valinehuolto-meilahti@helsinki.fi

When you send us a message, please remember to tell us which lab you are working in, which floor your lab is located on (including which house), and your contact information so that we can contact you if needed.

Items to be washed

Glassware and other items to be washed are picked up from the lab washbasin. Large items (such as incubator shelves) are also retrieved from the lab sink near the washbasin.

Always rinse dishes and other utensils before putting them to the washbasin. If the items are bloody or otherwise unrinsed, they will not be taken to the equipment maintenance unit for maintenance (occupational safety issue).

If the vessels contain solvents, it is the responsibility of the laboratory to evaporate the solvent.

The removal of gels and paraffin from vessels is the responsibility of the laboratory.

Tapes, labels, etc. should be removed from the containers in the laboratory.

Please, place the washbasin at an ergonomic height in the laboratory.

If you wish to have your dirty item returned sterilized, put it in the washbasin normally and inform us of the need for sterilization at our email address.

Common glassware (eg, erlenmeyer, decanters, measuring glasses, etc.) circulate throughout the house. We deliver the required number of glassware to the service points and general glass shelves. We return similar items, but we may not return the same item.

If the general glassware is worn or broken, we will replace it with a new one by equipment maintenance (applies only to general glassware, not laboratory’s ones).

Products to be sterilized

The products will be ready at the earliest the next day.

The expiry date of the product is indicated on the products. In the case of two dates, the earlier date is the date of sterilization.

Incubator shelves are sterilized only for old incubators that do not have a decontamination program. (Of course, we wash the shelves of all incubators).

Certain items are automatically sterilized after washing, for example, ultracentrifuge tubes and instruments. These products are marked by equipment maintenance using autoclavable marking tapes.

Do the following:

If you need general glassware to be sterilized (eg, Erlenmeyers, etc.), send us a service request by email. We will bring you the product you requested, as soon as we can, to the service point nearest you.

If you wish to have your clean items (eg, Eppendorf tubes, etc.) back sterilized, bring them in sterilizing bags to the nearest service point.

Fill the bag only up to 60%. Otherwise, steam may not disperse around all products and sterilize them properly. Also, the bag must have space for the seams.

The text should be at the end of the bag, i.e., outside the seams, not in the sterile region

Be sure to send the tip boxes to be washed from time to time.

Avoid the following:

Putting dirty items needing to be washed among clean things to be sterilized, because we have to assume that all items are contaminated and rewash them all.

Putting a clean product to be sterilized (eg, sterilization bag) in a dirty washbasin. The product is contaminated, and we have to dispose of the bag and its goods in the trash.

To bag a dirty product in an autoclave bag. Please follow the instructions above. We assume that the bagged items are clean and ready for sterilization.

Liquids to be sterilized

Bring all liquids to be sterilized to the service point, where the service personnel will retrieve them.

Always mark on the bottle what the solution is. The personnel will not autoclave unknown liquids. (Quality and safety issue). Also, mark who’s it is (Lab name and location, for example, lab A, 2U, 3floor) so that we know where to return it.

Fill bottles with only 70% of the bottle volume. Otherwise, the liquids will boil over in the autoclave.

Critical concentration solutions, such as 10XPBS can be filled up to 85% volume, because they are run with a slower, more water-consuming program. Before the delivery of the bottles for sterilization, contact us to discuss the matter via our email.

DEPC waters and DEPC-flushed, bagged products are also brought to a service point. They are not picked up by laboratory technicians from fume hoods. (DEPC evaporates CO2 and ethanol in small amounts so that they can be safely taken to a service point).

Biomedicum 1 | Biomedicum 1 | Biomedicum 2U | Biomedicum 2U
 | | | 
1st Floor | B104a ja B105a | P-Floor | GP13a
2nd | B204a ja B205a | 2nd | S208.2 + end of hallway
3rd | Folkhälsan Equipment maintenance | 3rd | E306b
4th | B404a ja B405a1 | Minerva | EP02a
5th | B504a ja B505a | Lastenklinikka laboratory | E104a
(the old maintenance unit on all BM1 floors) | (the old maintenance unit on all BM1 floors) | (the old maintenance unit on all BM1 floors) |
